# Welcome to pytest-django!

pytest-django allows you to test your Django project/applications with the
[pytest testing tool](https://pytest.org/).

- [Quick start / tutorial](https://pytest-django.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorial.html)

- [Changelog](https://pytest-django.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelog.html)

- Full documentation: <https://pytest-django.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>

- [Contribution docs](https://pytest-django.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing.html)

- Version compatibility:

  - Django: 4.2, 5.0, 5.1 and latest main branch (compatible at the time
    of each release)
  - Python: CPython>=3.8 or PyPy 3
  - pytest: >=7.0

  For compatibility with older versions, use previous pytest-django releases.

- Licence: BSD

- [All contributors](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django/contributors)

- GitHub repository: <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django>

- [Issue tracker](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django/issues)

- [Python Package Index (PyPI)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytest-django/)

## Install pytest-django

```
pip install pytest-django
```

## Why would I use this instead of Django's `manage.py test` command?

Running your test suite with pytest-django allows you to tap into the features
that are already present in pytest. Here are some advantages:

- [Manage test dependencies with pytest fixtures.](https://pytest.org/en/latest/how-to/fixtures.html)
- Less boilerplate tests: no need to import unittest, create a subclass with methods. Write tests as regular functions.
- Database re-use: no need to re-create the test database for every test run.
- Run tests in multiple processes for increased speed (with the pytest-xdist plugin).
- Make use of other [pytest plugins](https://pytest.org/en/latest/how-to/plugins.html).
- Works with both worlds: Existing unittest-style TestCase's still work without any modifications.

See the [pytest documentation](https://pytest.org/en/latest/) for more information on pytest itself.

don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: pytest_django.lazy_django
documentation: Helpers to load Django lazily when Django settings can't be configured.

functions.name: pytest_django.lazy_django.skip_if_no_django
documentation: Raises a skip exception when no Django settings are available

functions.name: pytest_django.lazy_django.django_settings_is_configured
documentation:
Return whether the Django settings module has been configured.

This uses either the DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE environment variable, or the
configured flag in the Django settings object if django.conf has already
been imported.

modules.name: pytest_django.plugin
documentation:
A pytest plugin which helps testing Django applications

This plugin handles creating and destroying the test environment and
test database and provides some useful text fixtures.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.pytest_collectstart
documentation: Convert Django test tags on test classes to pytest marks.
Unlike the Django test runner, we only check tags on Django
test classes, to keep the plugin's effect contained.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.pytest_itemcollected
documentation: Convert Django test tags on test methods to pytest marks.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.django_test_environment
documentation:
Setup Django's test environment for the testing session.

XXX It is a little dodgy that this is an autouse fixture.  Perhaps
    an email fixture should be requested in order to be able to
    use the Django email machinery just like you need to request a
    db fixture for access to the Django database, etc.  But
    without duplicating a lot more of Django's test support code
    we need to follow this model.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.django_db_blocker
documentation:
Wrapper around Django's database access.

This object can be used to re-enable database access.  This fixture is used
internally in pytest-django to build the other fixtures and can be used for
special database handling.

The object is a context manager and provides the methods
.unblock()/.block() and .restore() to temporarily enable database access.

This is an advanced feature that is meant to be used to implement database
fixtures.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._django_db_marker
documentation: Implement the django_db marker, internal to pytest-django.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._django_setup_unittest
documentation: Setup a django unittest, internal to pytest-django.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.mailoutbox
documentation: A clean email outbox to which Django-generated emails are sent.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.django_mail_patch_dns
documentation: Patch the server dns name used in email messages.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.django_mail_dnsname
documentation: Return server dns name for using in email messages.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._django_set_urlconf
documentation: Apply the @pytest.mark.urls marker, internal to pytest-django.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._fail_for_invalid_template_variable
documentation:
Fixture that fails for invalid variables in templates.

This fixture will fail each test that uses django template rendering
should a template contain an invalid template variable.
The fail message will include the name of the invalid variable and
in most cases the template name.

It does not raise an exception, but fails, as the stack trace doesn't
offer any helpful information to debug.
This behavior can be switched off using the marker:
``pytest.mark.ignore_template_errors``

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._template_string_if_invalid_marker
documentation:
Apply the @pytest.mark.ignore_template_errors marker,
internal to pytest-django.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin._django_clear_site_cache
documentation:
Clears `django.contrib.sites.models.SITE_CACHE` to avoid
unexpected behavior with cached site objects.

functions.name: pytest_django.plugin.validate_urls
documentation:
Validate the urls marker.

It checks the signature and creates the `urls` attribute on the
marker which will have the correct value.

documentation: Enable access to the Django database.

methods.name: pytest_django.plugin.DjangoDbBlocker.block

Manager for django.db.backends.base.base.BaseDatabaseWrapper.

This is the object returned by django_db_blocker.

modules.name: pytest_django.fixtures
documentation: All pytest-django fixtures

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_modify_db_settings
documentation: Modify db settings just before the databases are configured.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_use_migrations
documentation: Return whether to use migrations to create the test databases.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_keepdb
documentation: Return whether to re-use an existing database and to keep it after the test run.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_createdb
documentation: Return whether the database is to be re-created before running any tests.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_setup
documentation: Top level fixture to ensure test databases are available

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.validate_django_db
documentation:
Validate the django_db marker.

It checks the signature and creates the `transaction`,
`reset_sequences`, `databases`, `serialized_rollback` and
`available_apps` attributes on the marker which will have the correct
values.

Sequence reset, serialized_rollback, and available_apps are only allowed
when combined with transaction.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.db
documentation:
Require a django test database.

This database will be setup with the default fixtures and will have
the transaction management disabled. At the end of the test the outer
transaction that wraps the test itself will be rolled back to undo any
changes to the database (in case the backend supports transactions).
This is more limited than the `transactional_db` fixture but
faster.

If both `db` and `transactional_db` are requested,
`transactional_db` takes precedence.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.transactional_db
documentation:
Require a django test database with transaction support.

This will re-initialise the django database for each test and is
thus slower than the normal `db` fixture.

If you want to use the database with transactions you must request
this resource.

If both `db` and `transactional_db` are requested,
`transactional_db` takes precedence.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_reset_sequences
documentation:
Require a transactional test database with sequence reset support.

This requests the `transactional_db` fixture, and additionally
enforces a reset of all auto increment sequences. If the enquiring
test relies on such values (e.g. ids as primary keys), you should
request this resource to ensure they are consistent across tests.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_db_serialized_rollback
documentation:
Require a test database with serialized rollbacks.

This requests the `db` fixture, and additionally performs rollback
emulation - serializes the database contents during setup and restores
it during teardown.

This fixture may be useful for transactional tests, so is usually combined
with `transactional_db`, but can also be useful on databases which do not
support transactions.

Note that this will slow down that test suite by approximately 3x.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.client
documentation: A Django test client instance.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.async_client
documentation: A Django test async client instance.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_user_model
documentation: The class of Django's user model.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_username_field
documentation: The fieldname for the username used with Django's user model.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.admin_user
documentation:
A Django admin user.

This uses an existing user with username "admin", or creates a new one with
password "password".

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.admin_client
documentation: A Django test client logged in as an admin user.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.rf
documentation: RequestFactory instance

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.async_rf
documentation: AsyncRequestFactory instance

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.settings
documentation: A Django settings object which restores changes after the testrun

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.live_server
documentation:
Run a live Django server in the background during tests

The address the server is started from is taken from the
--liveserver command line option or if this is not provided from
the DJANGO_LIVE_TEST_SERVER_ADDRESS environment variable. If
neither is provided `localhost` is used. See the Django
documentation for its full syntax.

NOTE: If the live server needs database access to handle a request

: your test will have to request database access. Furthermore
  when the tests want to see data added by the live-server (or
  the other way around) transactional database access will be
  needed as data inside a transaction is not shared between
  the live server and test code.

  Static assets will be automatically served when
  `django.contrib.staticfiles` is available in INSTALLED_APPS.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures._live_server_helper
documentation:
Helper to make live_server work, internal to pytest-django.

This helper will dynamically request the transactional_db fixture
for a test which uses the live_server fixture.  This allows the
server and test to access the database without having to mark
this explicitly which is handy since it is usually required and
matches the Django behaviour.

The separate helper is required since live_server can not request
transactional_db directly since it is session scoped instead of
function-scoped.

It will also override settings only for the duration of the test.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_assert_num_queries
documentation: Allows to check for an expected number of DB queries.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_assert_max_num_queries
documentation: Allows to check for an expected maximum number of DB queries.

functions.name: pytest_django.fixtures.django_capture_on_commit_callbacks
documentation: Captures transaction.on_commit() callbacks for the given database connection.

classes.name: pytest_django.fixtures.DjangoAssertNumQueries
documentation:
The type of the `django_assert_num_queries` and
`django_assert_max_num_queries` fixtures.

classes.name: pytest_django.fixtures.DjangoCaptureOnCommitCallbacks
documentation: The type of the `django_capture_on_commit_callbacks` fixture.
